need: Ingest Thomas Bloom's complete Erdős problems catalog into aporia/mathematics/questions.jsonl
target_schema: aporia/mathematics/questions.jsonl (append, MATH-NNNN id continuation), with new field `bloom_id` preserving the canonical 'Erdős Problem #N' reference and `bloom_status` preserving the curator-tracked status (open/solved/partially-solved)
scope: ~800-1000 problems (exact count not confirmable from outside; site bounced fetch with 403 Cloudflare gating)
context: Current Erdős coverage in questions.jsonl is 15 entries from Wikipedia's surface list — roughly 1-2% of Bloom's catalog. Required as sample frame for Maieutēs probe candidate M-2026-04-26-001 (operator-transfer base-rate measurement on classical conjectures, per stoa/discussions/2026-04-26-aporia-on-amateur-vibe-maths-erdos-result.md). Also feeds aporia/calibration/battery_calibration.jsonl with both true-positive anchors (Bloom-marked solved problems) and live-target open questions. Reinforces feedback_tensor_first because Erdős problems span what humans call number theory, combinatorics, graph theory, Diophantine analysis — perfect material for operator-derived structural partitioning.
known_issues: 403 Cloudflare gating on direct fetch (same pattern Mnemosyne already routes around for OEIS). Design for polite scraping with backoff per feedback_rate_limits; build a local mirror; do not hammer the site. Bloom curates manually so the corpus is stable; one good ingest pass plus quarterly refresh should suffice.

need: Ingest MIT CSAIL + KAUST + HUMAIN's MathNet dataset — 30,676 olympiad problems from 47 countries with peer-reviewed multi-page expert solutions, in 17 languages, spanning ~40 years of competitions
source: MIT CSAIL (released 2026-04-20). Specific download URL not yet confirmed in coverage; check https://www.csail.mit.edu/ or contact authors. License stated as 'free to public' but specific OSI license type not yet documented — verify before mirror commit.
target_schema: Append to aporia/mathematics/questions.jsonl with new fields: `mathnet_id` (canonical problem ID preserved), `competition` (IMO/national/regional + year), `country_origin`, `language_original` + `language_english_translation_available` flags, `difficulty_tier`, `solution_paradigm_tags` (list of P01..P22 that the expert solution invokes — THIS is the calibration-corpus value, manually-extractable from the multi-page solutions during ingest), `solution_text` (separate file or column due to length).
scope: 30,676 problems + multi-page solutions; estimated few GB compressed. Basement-feasible.
urgency: high — second-priority data ingest after REQ-001 (Bloom-Erdős). MathNet provides 30K problem-paradigm-solution triples for the calibration corpus. Currently aporia/calibration/battery_calibration.jsonl has only 2 anchors. MathNet expands by 4 orders of magnitude.
context: Surfaced by James 2026-04-26. The high-value field is `solution_paradigm_tags` — every MathNet solution names the paradigm(s) used to solve it (P01 algebraic translation / P03 symmetry / P11 sieve / P17 variational / etc.). This is direct calibration anchor data for the 18+2 paradigm catalog. Also feeds: (a) P22 ML-Saliency-Guided Conjecture (DJLT-style learned-predictor training corpus); (b) genealogy routine extension (40 years of olympiad-scale solution-paradigm pairs vs the current focus on 20-year computational breakthroughs); (c) cross-language structure (17 languages — first multilingual corpus in the substrate). Caveat: olympiad problems are 'clever-trick-on-known-solvable' style, distinct from the substrate's primary 'operator-driven structural-region exploration of open conjectures' mode. MathNet is RICH for paradigm-classification calibration; LESS rich as raw substrate for cross-region empirical sweeps. Per `feedback_tensor_first`: ingest yes; train no — Apollo/Rhea remains deferred until closed-loop condition met. James 2026-04-26 framing: 'calibration ticks are important. Finding more like them in deeper landscapes and dimensions will be key' — prioritize calibration-anchor corpora as a recurring substrate need, not just MathNet.
known_issues: License must be confirmed OSI-compatible before local mirror is committed to git. Multi-language solutions need translation pipeline or English-only filter for first-pass calibration use. Paradigm-tagging may need LLM-assisted extraction from solution prose; manual review by Kairos before adding to calibration corpus.
